   782→// HealthCheck queries the external cc-metric-store's health check endpoint.
   783→// It sends a HealthCheckReq as the request body to /api/healthcheck and
   784→// returns the per-node health check results.
   785→func (ccms *CCMetricStore) HealthCheck(cluster string,
   786→	nodes []string, metrics []string,
   787→) (map[string]metricstore.HealthCheckResult, error) {
   788→	req := metricstore.HealthCheckReq{
   789→		Cluster:     cluster,
   790→		Nodes:       nodes,
   791→		MetricNames: metrics,
   792→	}
   793→
   794→	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
   795→	if err := json.NewEncoder(buf).Encode(req); err != nil {
   796→		cclog.Errorf("Error while encoding health check request body: %s", err.Error())
   797→		return nil, err
   798→	}
   799→
   800→	endpoint := fmt.Sprintf("%s/api/healthcheck", ccms.url)
   801→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, endpoint, buf)
   802→	if err != nil {
   803→		cclog.Errorf("Error while building health check request: %s", err.Error())
   804→		return nil, err
   805→	}
   806→	if ccms.jwt != "" {
   807→		httpReq.Header.Add("Authorization", fmt.Sprintf("Bearer %s", ccms.jwt))
   808→	}
   809→
   810→	res, err := ccms.client.Do(httpReq)
   811→	if err != nil {
   812→		cclog.Errorf("Error while performing health check request: %s", err.Error())
   813→		return nil, err
   814→	}
   815→	defer res.Body.Close()
   816→
   817→	if res.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
   818→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("'%s': HTTP Status: %s", endpoint, res.Status)
   819→	}
   820→
   821→	var results map[string]metricstore.HealthCheckResult
   822→	if err := json.NewDecoder(bufio.NewReader(res.Body)).Decode(&results); err != nil {
   823→		cclog.Errorf("Error while decoding health check response: %s", err.Error())
   824→		return nil, err
   825→	}
   826→
   827→	return results, nil
   828→}
   829→
   830→// sanitizeStats replaces NaN values in statistics with 0 to enable JSON marshaling.
   831→// Regular float64 values cannot be JSONed when NaN.
   832→func sanitizeStats(avg, min, max *schema.Float) {
   833→	if avg.IsNaN() || min.IsNaN() || max.IsNaN() {
   834→		*avg = schema.Float(0)
   835→		*min = schema.Float(0)
   836→		*max = schema.Float(0)
   837→	}
   838→}
   839→
   840→// hasNaNStats returns true if any of the statistics contain NaN values.
   841→func hasNaNStats(avg, min, max schema.Float) bool {
   842→	return avg.IsNaN() || min.IsNaN() || max.IsNaN()
   843→}

     5→
     6→// This file implements ingestion of InfluxDB line-protocol metric data received
     7→// over NATS. Each line encodes one metric sample with the following structure:
     8→//
     9→//	<measurement>[,cluster=<c>][,hostname=<h>][,type=<t>][,type-id=<id>][,subtype=<s>][,stype-id=<id>] value=<v> [<timestamp>]
    10→//
    11→// The measurement name identifies the metric (e.g. "cpu_load"). Tags provide
    12→// routing information (cluster, host) and optional sub-device selectors (type,
    13→// subtype). Only one field is expected per line: "value".
    14→//
    15→// After decoding, each sample is:
    16→//  1. Written to the in-memory store via ms.WriteToLevel.
    17→//  2. If the checkpoint format is "wal", also forwarded to the WAL staging
    18→//     goroutine via the WALMessages channel for durable write-ahead logging.
    19→package metricstore
    20→
    21→import (
    22→	"bytes"
    23→	"context"
    24→	"fmt"
    25→	"sync"
    26→	"time"
    27→
    28→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    29→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/nats"
    30→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    31→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-line-protocol/v2/lineprotocol"
    32→)
    33→
    34→// ReceiveNats subscribes to all configured NATS subjects and feeds incoming
    35→// line-protocol messages into the MemoryStore.
    36→//
    37→// When workers > 1 a pool of goroutines drains a shared channel so that
    38→// multiple messages can be decoded in parallel. With workers == 1 the NATS
    39→// callback decodes inline (no channel overhead, lower latency).
    40→//
    41→// The function blocks until ctx is cancelled and all worker goroutines have
    42→// finished. It returns nil when the NATS client is not configured; callers
    43→// should treat that as a no-op rather than an error.
    44→func ReceiveNats(ms *MemoryStore,
    45→	workers int,
    46→	ctx context.Context,
    47→) error {
    48→	nc := nats.GetClient()
    49→
    50→	if nc == nil {
    51→		cclog.Warn("NATS client not initialized")
    52→		return nil
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	var wg sync.WaitGroup
    56→	msgs := make(chan []byte, workers*2)
    57→
    58→	for _, sc := range *Keys.Subscriptions {
    59→		clusterTag := sc.ClusterTag
    60→		if workers > 1 {
    61→			wg.Add(workers)
    62→
    63→			for range workers {
    64→				go func() {
    65→					defer wg.Done()
    66→					for m := range msgs {
    67→						dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(m)
    68→						if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    69→							cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    70→						}
    71→					}
    72→				}()
    73→			}
    74→
    75→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    76→				select {
    77→				case msgs <- data:
    78→				case <-ctx.Done():
    79→				}
    80→			})
    81→		} else {
    82→			nc.Subscribe(sc.SubscribeTo, func(subject string, data []byte) {
    83→				dec := lineprotocol.NewDecoderWithBytes(data)
    84→				if err := DecodeLine(dec, ms, clusterTag); err != nil {
    85→					cclog.Errorf("error: %s", err.Error())
    86→				}
    87→			})
    88→		}
    89→		cclog.Infof("NATS subscription to '%s' established", sc.SubscribeTo)
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	go func() {
    93→		<-ctx.Done()
    94→		close(msgs)
    95→	}()
    96→
    97→	wg.Wait()
    98→
    99→	return nil
   100→}

     6→// This file implements checkpoint persistence for the in-memory metric store.
     7→//
     8→// Checkpoints enable graceful restarts by periodically saving in-memory metric
     9→// data to disk in JSON or binary format. The checkpoint system:
    10→//
    11→// Key Features:
    12→//   - Periodic background checkpointing via the Checkpointing() worker
    13→//   - Two format families: JSON (human-readable) and WAL+binary (compact, crash-safe)
    14→//   - Parallel checkpoint creation and loading using worker pools
    15→//   - Hierarchical file organization: checkpoint_dir/cluster/host/timestamp.{json|bin}
    16→//   - WAL file: checkpoint_dir/cluster/host/current.wal (append-only, per-entry)
    17→//   - Only saves unarchived data (archived data is already persisted elsewhere)
    18→//   - GC optimization during loading to prevent excessive heap growth
    19→//
    20→// Checkpoint Workflow:
    21→//  1. Init() loads checkpoints within retention window at startup
    22→//  2. Checkpointing() worker periodically saves new data
    23→//  3. Shutdown() writes final checkpoint before exit
    24→//
    25→// File Organization:
    26→//
    27→//	checkpoints/
    28→//	  cluster1/
    29→//	    host001/
    30→//	      1234567890.json  (JSON format: full subtree snapshot)
    31→//	      1234567890.bin   (binary format: full subtree snapshot)
    32→//	      current.wal      (WAL format: append-only per-entry log)
    33→//	    host002/
    34→//	      ...

    36→
    37→import (
    38→	"bufio"
    39→	"context"
    40→	"encoding/json"
    41→	"errors"
    42→	"fmt"
    43→	"io/fs"

    45→	"path"

    52→	"time"

    55→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    56→)
    57→
    58→const (
    59→	CheckpointFilePerms = 0o644                    // File permissions for checkpoint files
    60→	CheckpointDirPerms  = 0o755                    // Directory permissions for checkpoint directories
    61→	GCTriggerInterval   = DefaultGCTriggerInterval // Interval for triggering GC during checkpoint loading
    62→)
    63→
    64→// CheckpointMetrics represents metric data in a checkpoint file.
    65→// Whenever the structure changes, update MarshalJSON as well!
    66→type CheckpointMetrics struct {
    67→	Data      []schema.Float `json:"data"`
    68→	Frequency int64          `json:"frequency"`

    71→
    72→// CheckpointFile represents the hierarchical structure of a checkpoint file.

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the package. Let me create a detailed summary report.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: `internal/metricstoreclient/` Package

### Overview

The `internal/metricstoreclient/` package is a **client library for querying cc-metric-store**, an external high-performance time series database optimized for HPC metric data. It mirrors the functionality of `pkg/metricstore/query.go` but communicates with an external cc-metric-store service via HTTP instead of using an in-memory store.

**Files in package:**
- `/Users/jan/prg/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricstoreclient/cc-metric-store.go` (28 KB) — Main client operations
- `/Users/jan/prg/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricstoreclient/cc-metric-store-queries.go` (16 KB) — Query building logic

---

### 1. Core Type: `CCMetricStore`

**Location:** `cc-metric-store.go`, lines 71-79

```go
type CCMetricStore struct {
    client        http.Client                 // HTTP client with 10-second timeout
    jwt           string                      // JWT Bearer token for authentication
    url           string                      // Base URL of cc-metric-store instance
    queryEndpoint string                      // Full URL to query API endpoint
    topologyCache map[string]*schema.Topology // cluster -> topology cache
}
```

**Purpose:** HTTP client for communicating with an external cc-metric-store service. Manages connection details, authentication (JWT), and caching of cluster topology information.

**Constructor:**
```go
func NewCCMetricStore(url string, token string) *CCMetricStore
```
- Creates client with 10-second timeout
- Initializes empty topology cache
- URL should include protocol and port (e.g., "http://localhost:8080")
- Token is JWT for Bearer authentication; empty string disables auth

---

### 2. API Request/Response Types

#### `APIQueryRequest` (lines 83-91)
Represents a request to the cc-metric-store query API:
```go
type APIQueryRequest struct {
    Cluster     string     // Target cluster name
    Queries     []APIQuery // Explicit list of metric queries
    ForAllNodes []string   // Metrics to query for all nodes (bulk query)
    From        int64      // Start time (Unix timestamp)
    To          int64      // End time (Unix timestamp)
    WithStats   bool       // Include min/avg/max statistics
    WithData    bool       // Include time series data points
}
```

#### `APIQuery` (lines 95-104)
Specifies a single metric query with optional scope filtering:
```go
type APIQuery struct {
    Type       *string  // Scope type (e.g., "core", "socket", "hwthread")
    SubType    *string  // Sub-scope type (reserved for future use)
    Metric     string   // Metric name
    Hostname   string   // Target hostname
    Resolution int      // Data resolution in seconds (0 = native)
    TypeIds    []string // IDs for the scope type (e.g., ["0", "1", "2"] for core IDs)
    SubTypeIds []string // IDs for sub-scope (reserved)
    Aggregate  bool     // Aggregate across TypeIds
}
```

#### `APIQueryResponse` (lines 108-111)
Contains results from a cc-metric-store query:
```go
type APIQueryResponse struct {
    Queries []APIQuery        // Echoed queries (for bulk requests)
    Results [][]APIMetricData // Result data, indexed by query
}
```

#### `APIMetricData` (lines 115-124)
Time series data and statistics for a single metric series:
```go
type APIMetricData struct {
    Error      *string        // Error message if query failed
    Data       []schema.Float // Time series data points
    From       int64          // Actual start time of data
    To         int64          // Actual end time of data
    Resolution int            // Actual resolution of data in seconds
    Avg        schema.Float   // Average value across time range
    Min        schema.Float   // Minimum value in time range
    Max        schema.Float   // Maximum value in time range
}
```

---

### 3. Core Methods on `CCMetricStore`

#### `doRequest()` (lines 144-188)
**Purpose:** Executes HTTP POST request to cc-metric-store query API

**Key behavior:**
- Encodes request body as JSON
- Adds JWT Bearer token if configured
- Adds API versioning (v2 for data with resampling, v1 without)
- Uses GET method with JSON body (unusual but matches cc-metric-store API)
- Returns parsed `APIQueryResponse`
- Properly closes response body to prevent resource leaks

#### `getTopology()` (lines 191-204)
**Purpose:** Returns topology for given cluster and subcluster, caching results

```go
func (ccms *CCMetricStore) getTopology(cluster, subCluster string) (*schema.Topology, error)
```

**Implementation:**
- Uses cache key `"cluster:subcluster"`
- Returns cached topology if available
- Otherwise loads via `archive.GetSubCluster()` and caches it
- Returns pointer to `schema.Topology` from subcluster

#### `getTopologyByNode()` (lines 207-214)
**Purpose:** Returns topology for given cluster and node, caching it


### 5. Query Building (cc-metric-store-queries.go)

#### Scope String Constants (lines 49-55)
Pre-converted strings to avoid repeated allocations:
```go
hwthreadString     = "hwthread"
coreString         = "core"
memoryDomainString = "memory_domain"
socketString       = "socket"
acceleratorString  = "accelerator"
```

#### `buildQueries()` (lines 67-149)
**Purpose:** Constructs API queries for job-specific metric data

**Signature:**
```go
func (ccms *CCMetricStore) buildQueries(
    job *schema.Job,
    metrics []string,
    scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    resolution int,
) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope, error)
```

**Returns:** Both queries and their corresponding assigned scopes (may differ from requested)

**Key logic:**
1. Validates metric configurations via `archive.GetMetricConfig()`
2. Skips metrics removed for subcluster
3. Deduplicates requested scopes to avoid redundant queries
4. For each host in job resources:
   - Uses job-allocated HWThreads (or full node if not specified)
   - Calls `buildScopeQueries()` for scope transformation
   - Accumulates queries and assigned scopes

**Optimization:** Tracks handled scopes to skip redundant queries

#### `buildNodeQueries()` (lines 161-252)
**Purpose:** Constructs API queries for node-specific metric data (Systems View)

**Signature:**
```go
func (ccms *CCMetricStore) buildNodeQueries(
    cluster string,
    subCluster string,
    nodes []string,
    metrics []string,
    scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    resolution int,
) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope, error)
```

**Key differences from `buildQueries()`:**
- Always uses full node topology (not job-specific subsets)
- Always uses all accelerators on each node
- Handles optional subCluster parameter:
  - If empty: calls `getTopologyByNode()` per node
  - If provided: calls `getTopology()` once and reuses

#### `buildScopeQueries()` (lines 257-517)
**Purpose:** Generates API queries for a given scope transformation

**Signature:**
```go
func buildScopeQueries(
    nativeScope, requestedScope schema.MetricScope,
    metric, hostname string,
    topology *schema.Topology,
    hwthreads []int,
    accelerators []string,
    resolution int,
) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope)
```

**Returns:** Empty slices for expected exceptions; nil for unexpected unhandled cases

**Scope Transformations Implemented (25+ cases):**

| Native → Requested | Behavior | Result Scope |
|---|---|---|
| Accelerator → Accelerator | Return as-is | Accelerator |
| Accelerator → Node | Aggregate all accelerators | Node |
| HWThread → HWThread | Return as-is | HWThread |
| HWThread → Core | Aggregate per core | Core (one query per core) |
| HWThread → Socket | Aggregate per socket | Socket (one query per socket) |
| HWThread → Node | Aggregate all threads | Node |
| Core → Core | Return as-is | Core |
| Core → Socket | Aggregate per socket | Socket (one query per socket) |
| Core → Node | Aggregate all cores | Node |
| MemoryDomain → MemoryDomain | Return as-is | MemoryDomain |
| MemoryDomain → Node | Aggregate all domains | Node |
| MemoryDomain → Socket | Aggregate domains per socket | Socket (one query per socket) |
| Socket → Socket | Return as-is | Socket |
| Socket → Node | Aggregate all sockets | Node |
| Node → Node | Return as-is | Node |

**Key features:**
- Uses `Aggregate: true` for aggregations, `false` for direct queries
- Sets `Type` and `TypeIds` for scope-specific queries
- Returns nil slices for unhandled cases (triggers error in `buildQueries()`)
- Uses helper functions like `GetCoresFromHWThreads()`, `GetSocketsFromCores()`, etc. from topology

#### `intToStringSlice()` (lines 521-527)
Helper function to convert integer hardware IDs to string format required by API:
```go
func intToStringSlice(is []int) []string
```

---

### 6. Helper Functions

#### `sanitizeStats()` (lines 832-838)
Replaces NaN values in statistics with 0 to enable JSON marshaling:
```go
func sanitizeStats(avg, min, max *schema.Float)
```

#### `hasNaNStats()` (lines 841-843)
Returns true if any of the statistics contain NaN values:
```go
func hasNaNStats(avg, min, max schema.Float) bool
```

---

### 7. Relationship to `pkg/metricstore/query.go`

Both packages implement **parallel interfaces** with nearly identical method signatures:

| Method | Location | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `LoadData()` | Both | Load job metric data with scope transformations |
| `LoadStats()` | Both | Load statistics at node scope |
| `LoadScopedStats()` | Both | Load statistics across multiple scopes |
| `HealthCheck()` | Both | Health check for metric store |

**Key differences:**

| Aspect | `pkg/metricstore/` | `internal/metricstoreclient/` |
|---|---|---|
| **Storage** | In-memory store | External cc-metric-store via HTTP |
| **Data fetching** | `FetchData()` (internal) | `doRequest()` (HTTP to cc-metric-store) |
| **Query builder** | `buildQueries()` in query.go | `buildQueries()` + `buildScopeQueries()` in cc-metric-store-queries.go |
| **Authentication** | None (in-process) | JWT Bearer token |
| **Caching** | No caching | Topology caching |
| **API versioning** | Not applicable | Uses cc-metric-store v2 API |
| **Timeout** | Not applicable | 10-second HTTP timeout |
| **Resolution type** | `int64` | `int` |

**Code Similarity:**
- Both use same scope transformation logic
- Both construct queries with metric configs from `archive.GetMetricConfig()`
- Both handle partial errors (return data for successful queries)
- Both sanitize NaN statistics
- Nearly identical result processing loops with identical safety checks

**When to use each:**
- **`pkg/metricstore/`**: When cc-metric-store is co-located or unavailable
- **`internal/metricstoreclient/`**: When cc-metric-store is available externally (production preferred for scalability)

---

### 8. API Endpoint Details

**Query Endpoint:**
- Base: `http://cc-metric-store-host:port`
- Query: `{base}/api/query?version=v2`
- Health: `{base}/api/healthcheck`

**Message Format:**
Uses InfluxDB-like line protocol with JSON payloads in `APIQuery` structures. For job events/node updates via NATS (per CLAUDE.md), uses InfluxDB line protocol directly.

---

### 9. Error Handling

- **Partial errors**: Methods return both data and error; error lists failed queries
- **Index out of range**: Prevented with bounds checking; logged as warnings
- **Invalid scopes**: Return nil slices from `buildScopeQueries()` triggering error
- **Metric config missing**: Logged warning, metric skipped
- **HTTP errors**: Returned from `doRequest()`
- **Context cancellation**: Supported via context parameter in all load methods

---

### Summary

The `internal/metricstoreclient/` package is a **complete HTTP client for external cc-metric-store** that mirrors the functionality of the internal metric store while communicating via HTTP API. It handles complex scope transformations, topology caching, and partial error reporting. The query building logic handles 25+ scope transformation cases, enabling flexible metric data requests across different hardware granularities while maintaining compatibility with the in-memory metric store interface.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: `internal/metricstoreclient/` Package

### Overview

The `internal/metricstoreclient/` package is a **client library for querying cc-metric-store**, an external high-performance time series database optimized for HPC metric data. It mirrors the functionality of `pkg/metricstore/query.go` but communicates with an external cc-metric-store service via HTTP instead of using an in-memory store.

**Files in package:**
- `/Users/jan/prg/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricstoreclient/cc-metric-store.go` (28 KB) — Main client operations
- `/Users/jan/prg/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricstoreclient/cc-metric-store-queries.go` (16 KB) — Query building logic

---

### 1. Core Type: `CCMetricStore`

**Location:** `cc-metric-store.go`, lines 71-79

```go
type CCMetricStore struct {
    client        http.Client                 // HTTP client with 10-second timeout
    jwt           string                      // JWT Bearer token for authentication
    url           string                      // Base URL of cc-metric-store instance
    queryEndpoint string                      // Full URL to query API endpoint
    topologyCache map[string]*schema.Topology // cluster -> topology cache
}
```

**Purpose:** HTTP client for communicating with an external cc-metric-store service. Manages connection details, authentication (JWT), and caching of cluster topology information.

**Constructor:**
```go
func NewCCMetricStore(url string, token string) *CCMetricStore
```
- Creates client with 10-second timeout
- Initializes empty topology cache
- URL should include protocol and port (e.g., "http://localhost:8080")
- Token is JWT for Bearer authentication; empty string disables auth

---

### 2. API Request/Response Types

#### `APIQueryRequest` (lines 83-91)
Represents a request to the cc-metric-store query API:
```go
type APIQueryRequest struct {
    Cluster     string     // Target cluster name
    Queries     []APIQuery // Explicit list of metric queries
    ForAllNodes []string   // Metrics to query for all nodes (bulk query)
    From        int64      // Start time (Unix timestamp)
    To          int64      // End time (Unix timestamp)
    WithStats   bool       // Include min/avg/max statistics
    WithData    bool       // Include time series data points
}
```

#### `APIQuery` (lines 95-104)
Specifies a single metric query with optional scope filtering:
```go
type APIQuery struct {
    Type       *string  // Scope type (e.g., "core", "socket", "hwthread")
    SubType    *string  // Sub-scope type (reserved for future use)
    Metric     string   // Metric name
    Hostname   string   // Target hostname
    Resolution int      // Data resolution in seconds (0 = native)
    TypeIds    []string // IDs for the scope type (e.g., ["0", "1", "2"] for core IDs)
    SubTypeIds []string // IDs for sub-scope (reserved)
    Aggregate  bool     // Aggregate across TypeIds
}
```

#### `APIQueryResponse` (lines 108-111)
Contains results from a cc-metric-store query:
```go
type APIQueryResponse struct {
    Queries []APIQuery        // Echoed queries (for bulk requests)
    Results [][]APIMetricData // Result data, indexed by query
}
```

#### `APIMetricData` (lines 115-124)
Time series data and statistics for a single metric series:
```go
type APIMetricData struct {
    Error      *string        // Error message if query failed
    Data       []schema.Float // Time series data points
    From       int64          // Actual start time of data
    To         int64          // Actual end time of data
    Resolution int            // Actual resolution of data in seconds
    Avg        schema.Float   // Average value across time range
    Min        schema.Float   // Minimum value in time range
    Max        schema.Float   // Maximum value in time range
}
```

## 2. HOW FetchData WORKS

**Function Signature**: `func FetchData(req APIQueryRequest) (*APIQueryResponse, error)`

**High-Level Flow**:

1. **Validation**
   - Checks `req.From <= req.To`
   - Ensures cluster is specified if `ForAllNodes` is used
   - Verifies MemoryStore is initialized

2. **Query Expansion**
   - If `req.ForAllNodes` is set: auto-generates queries for all nodes in the cluster
   - Calls `ms.ListChildren([]string{cluster})` to get node list
   - Creates one query per (node, metric) combination

3. **Selector Construction** (per query)
   - Base selector: `[cluster, hostname]`
   - If `Aggregate=true` or no Type specified:
     - Creates single aggregated selector
     - If Type is specified and has multiple TypeIds:
       - Uses `SelectorElement{Group: ids}` for multi-selection
     - Similar for SubType
   - If `Aggregate=false`:
     - Creates individual selector for each Type/SubType ID combination
     - Enables non-aggregated per-component results

4. **Data Fetching**
   - For each selector: calls `ms.Read(selector, metric, from, to, resolution)`
   - Returns: `(data, from, to, resolution, error)`

5. **Result Processing** (per query result)
   - **Skips** `ErrNoHostOrMetric` errors (logs warning, continues)
   - **Includes** other errors in `APIMetricData.Error` field
   - If `req.WithStats`: calls `data.AddStats()` to compute avg/min/max
   - If `query.ScaleFactor != 0`: calls `data.ScaleBy()` for unit conversion
   - If `req.WithPadding`: calls `data.PadDataWithNull()` to align timestamps
   - If `!req.WithData`: clears the data array (keeps stats)

6. **Response Assembly**
   - Returns 2D array: `Results[query_idx][selector_idx]`
   - Each inner element is `APIMetricData` with data/stats

**Example Scenario**:
```
Request: Fetch cpu_load for cluster "emmy", node "node001", all cores (0-3)
  Type="core", TypeIds=["0","1","2","3"], Aggregate=false

Result: 4 separate APIMetricData entries, one per core
  (each with separate data points)
```

---

## 3. InternalMetricStore & Its Relationship

### Purpose
`InternalMetricStore` is a **public facade** that abstracts the `MemoryStore` singleton, preventing direct exposure of internal details.

### Key Methods

**`LoadData()`** (query.go)
```go
func (ccms *InternalMetricStore) LoadData(
    job *schema.Job,
    metrics []string,
    scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    ctx context.Context,
    resolution int,
) (schema.JobData, error)
```

**Responsibilities**:
1. Calls `buildQueries()` to construct API queries with scope transformation
2. Determines target scope for each query (e.g., HWThread native → Socket requested)
3. Calls `FetchData()` to retrieve metric data
4. Reorganizes results by metric and scope into `schema.JobData` structure
5. Returns: `map[metricName]map[scope]*JobMetric`

**Scope Transformation Logic** (in `buildQueries()`):
- Native scope >= requested scope: **Aggregate** data (Aggregate=true)
- Native scope < requested scope: **Error** (cannot refine granularity)
- Handles special cases:
  - Accelerator metrics (no aggregation)
  - Memory domain metrics
  - Multi-node jobs (per-node queries)

**`HealthCheck()`** (query.go)
```go
func (ccms *InternalMetricStore) HealthCheck(
    cluster string,
    nodes []string,
    metrics []string,
) (map[string]HealthCheckResult, error)
```
- Delegates to `ms.HealthCheck()`
- Returns monitoring state per node (Full/Partial/Failed)

### Relationship to MemoryStore

| Aspect | InternalMetricStore | MemoryStore |
|--------|---------------------|------------|
| **Role** | Public API facade | Internal storage/retrieval |
| **Instance** | Singleton (var MetricStoreHandle) | Singleton (msInstance) |
| **Access** | Used by external packages | Only via InternalMetricStore |
| **Methods** | High-level: LoadData, HealthCheck | Low-level: Write, Read, Free |
| **Scope** | Job-centric, scope transformation | Metric-centric, raw storage |

---

## 4. KEY DATA STRUCTURES & RELATIONSHIPS

### Hierarchical Tree Organization

```
MemoryStore
└── root (Level)
    └── children["emmy"] (cluster Level)
        └── metrics [buffer, buffer, ...] (node-level aggregates)
        └── children["node001"] (host Level)
            └── metrics [buffer, buffer, ...]
            └── children["socket0"] (Level)
                └── metrics [buffer, buffer, ...]
                └── children["core0"] (Level)
                    └── metrics [buffer, buffer, ...] (actual data)
                └── children["core1"]
                    └── metrics [buffer, buffer, ...]
```

### Buffer Chain (Linked List)

Time ordering: oldest ← prev -- prev -- newest (head)

```
[start=1000] ← [start=1512] ← [start=2024] (head)
 data[512]       data[512]      data[42]
```

### Metrics Indexing

- Each `Level.metrics` is a slice of `*buffer`
- Index = `MetricConfig.offset` (assigned during `InitMetrics()`)
- Example:
  ```
  Metrics map: {"cpu_load": {offset: 0}, "mem_used": {offset: 1}}
  Level.metrics: [*buffer for cpu_load, *buffer for mem_used]
  ```

---

## 5. LIFECYCLE & BACKGROUND WORKERS

### Initialization (Init)
1. Decodes JSON config into `Keys`
2. Calls `InitMetrics()` to create singleton MemoryStore
3. Loads checkpoints from disk within retention window
4. Starts 5 background workers:
   - `Retention()` - Frees old data based on retention policy
   - `Checkpointing()` - Periodic snapshot to disk
   - `CleanUp()` - Archives/deletes data older than retention
   - `WALStaging()` - Writes WAL entries to disk (if WAL format)
   - `MemoryUsageTracker()` - Monitors memory, triggers emergency freeing

### Shutdown
1. Cancels all background workers
2. Closes WAL messages channel (if WAL format)
3. Writes final checkpoint to disk

---

## 6. CHECKPOINT FORMATS

### JSON Format (Legacy)
```
checkpoints/
  cluster1/
    host001/
      1234567890.json
```

Structure:
```go
type CheckpointFile struct {
    Metrics  map[string]*CheckpointMetrics
    Children map[string]*CheckpointFile
    From     int64
    To       int64
}
```

### WAL Format (New, Recommended)
```
checkpoints/
  cluster1/
    host001/
      1234567890.bin    (full binary snapshot)
      current.wal       (append-only write-ahead log)
```

- **Binary snapshot**: Complete tree dump at a point in time
- **WAL file**: Per-write entries, replayed on startup for crash recovery
- **Rotation**: After checkpoint, WAL is truncated

---

## 7. CONCURRENCY STRATEGY

### RWMutex Usage
- **Read-heavy**: Metric reads during `ms.Read()` use `RLock`
- **Write-rare**: Level creation and buffer writes use `Lock`
- **Double-checked locking**: In `findLevelOrCreate()` prevents race conditions

### Channel-based Communication
- `WALMessages` channel: Queues WAL entries for disk write
- `WALStaging()` goroutine: Drains channel, batches to disk

### Buffer Pool
- Sync.Pool reduces GC pressure
- `PersistentBufferPool` with LRU cleanup (based on lastUsed timestamp)

---

## 8. RETENTION & MEMORY MANAGEMENT

### Retention Policy
- Time-based: Data older than `RetentionInMemory` is freed
- **NodeProvider integration**: Preserves data for nodes with active jobs
- Runs at half the retention interval

### Memory Emergency
- Monitors actual heap usage via `runtime.MemStats`
- If exceeds `MemoryCap`: triggers `ForceFree()` up to 100 iterations
- `ForceFree()` removes oldest buffer from each metric chain

### Free Mechanisms
- **Free()**: Removes buffers older than threshold, respecting active nodes
- **FreeSelected()**: Frees specific selectors (excludes others)
- **ForceFree()**: Unconditionally removes oldest buffers

---

## Summary

The `pkg/metricstore/` package is a production-grade time-series storage system with:
- **Hierarchical organization** for efficient per-component metric storage
- **Flexible API** supporting both raw queries and job-centric scope transformation
- **Robust persistence** via JSON or WAL+binary checkpoint formats
- **Intelligent memory management** with retention, emergency freeing, and pooling
- **Real-time ingestion** via NATS line-protocol
- **Health monitoring** with degraded metric detection

The **InternalMetricStore** abstraction provides a clean public interface for job metric loading, handling scope transformation automatically.

4. Starts 5 background workers:
   - `Retention()` - Frees old data based on retention policy
   - `Checkpointing()` - Periodic snapshot to disk
   - `CleanUp()` - Archives/deletes data older than retention
   - `WALStaging()` - Writes WAL entries to disk (if WAL format)
   - `MemoryUsageTracker()` - Monitors memory, triggers emergency freeing

### Shutdown
1. Cancels all background workers
2. Closes WAL messages channel (if WAL format)
3. Writes final checkpoint to disk

---

## 6. CHECKPOINT FORMATS

### JSON Format (Legacy)
```
checkpoints/
  cluster1/
    host001/
      1234567890.json
```

Structure:
```go
type CheckpointFile struct {
    Metrics  map[string]*CheckpointMetrics
    Children map[string]*CheckpointFile
    From     int64
    To       int64
}
```

### WAL Format (New, Recommended)
```
checkpoints/
  cluster1/
    host001/
      1234567890.bin    (full binary snapshot)
      current.wal       (append-only write-ahead log)
```

- **Binary snapshot**: Complete tree dump at a point in time
- **WAL file**: Per-write entries, replayed on startup for crash recovery
- **Rotation**: After checkpoint, WAL is truncated

---

## 7. CONCURRENCY STRATEGY

### RWMutex Usage
- **Read-heavy**: Metric reads during `ms.Read()` use `RLock`
- **Write-rare**: Level creation and buffer writes use `Lock`
- **Double-checked locking**: In `findLevelOrCreate()` prevents race conditions

### Channel-based Communication
- `WALMessages` channel: Queues WAL entries for disk write
- `WALStaging()` goroutine: Drains channel, batches to disk

### Buffer Pool
- Sync.Pool reduces GC pressure
- `PersistentBufferPool` with LRU cleanup (based on lastUsed timestamp)

---

## 8. RETENTION & MEMORY MANAGEMENT

### Retention Policy
- Time-based: Data older than `RetentionInMemory` is freed
- **NodeProvider integration**: Preserves data for nodes with active jobs
- Runs at half the retention interval

### Memory Emergency
- Monitors actual heap usage via `runtime.MemStats`
- If exceeds `MemoryCap`: triggers `ForceFree()` up to 100 iterations
- `ForceFree()` removes oldest buffer from each metric chain

### Free Mechanisms
- **Free()**: Removes buffers older than threshold, respecting active nodes
- **FreeSelected()**: Frees specific selectors (excludes others)
- **ForceFree()**: Unconditionally removes oldest buffers

---

## Summary

The `pkg/metricstore/` package is a production-grade time-series storage system with:
- **Hierarchical organization** for efficient per-component metric storage
- **Flexible API** supporting both raw queries and job-centric scope transformation
- **Robust persistence** via JSON or WAL+binary checkpoint formats
- **Intelligent memory management** with retention, emergency freeing, and pooling
- **Real-time ingestion** via NATS line-protocol
- **Health monitoring** with degraded metric detection

The **InternalMetricStore** abstraction provides a clean public interface for job metric loading, handling scope transformation automatically.

     6→// Package metricstoreclient provides a client for querying the cc-metric-store time series database.

    11→//
    12→// # Architecture
    13→//
    14→// The package is split into two main components:
    15→//   - Client Operations (cc-metric-store.go): HTTP client, request handling, data loading methods
    16→//   - Query Building (cc-metric-store-queries.go): Query construction and scope transformation logic
    17→//
    18→// # Basic Usage
    19→//

    30→// The client supports hierarchical metric scopes that map to HPC hardware topology:
    31→//   - MetricScopeAccelerator: GPU/accelerator level metrics
    32→//   - MetricScopeHWThread: Hardware thread (SMT) level metrics
    33→//   - MetricScopeCore: CPU core level metrics
    34→//   - MetricScopeSocket: CPU socket level metrics
    35→//   - MetricScopeMemoryDomain: NUMA domain level metrics
    36→//   - MetricScopeNode: Full node level metrics
    37→//
    38→// The client automatically handles scope transformations, aggregating finer-grained metrics
    39→// to coarser scopes when needed (e.g., aggregating core metrics to socket level).
    40→//
    41→// # Error Handling

    43→// The client supports partial errors - if some queries fail, it returns both the successful
    44→// data and an error listing the failed queries. This allows processing partial results
    45→// when some nodes or metrics are temporarily unavailable.

    47→// # API Versioning

    49→// The client uses cc-metric-store API v2, which includes support for:

I need to design a plan to synchronize and consolidate the query-building logic between two packages that implement the same MetricDataRepository interface:

## Context

1. **`pkg/metricstore/query.go`** (InternalMetricStore) — in-memory metric store
2. **`internal/metricstoreclient/cc-metric-store-queries.go`** (CCMetricStore) — HTTP client to external cc-metric-store

Both implement the `MetricDataRepository` interface from `internal/metricdispatch/metricdata.go`.

## Key Differences Found

### buildQueries differences:
- **pkg/metricstore**: Inline scope transformation (long chain of if-statements in the inner loop). Uses `map[schema.MetricScope]bool` for dedup. Resolution is `int64`.
- **metricstoreclient**: Extracted `buildScopeQueries()` helper function. Uses labeled loop + `[]schema.MetricScope` slice for dedup. Resolution is `int`. Has topology caching via `getTopology()`.

### buildScopeQueries (only in metricstoreclient):
- Clean standalone function handling all scope transformations
- Returns empty slices for expected exceptions, nil for unhandled cases
- Includes **MemoryDomain → Socket** transformation (MISSING from pkg/metricstore)

### buildNodeQueries differences:
- **pkg/metricstore**: Gets topology inline per node. Uses `map[schema.MetricScope]bool` dedup. Has inline scope transformation.
- **metricstoreclient**: Uses `getTopology()`/`getTopologyByNode()` with caching. Uses labeled loop dedup. Delegates to `buildScopeQueries()`.

### APIQuery type differences:
- **pkg/metricstore**: `Resolution int64`, has `ScaleFactor schema.Float`
- **metricstoreclient**: `Resolution int`, no ScaleFactor

### sanitizeStats differences:
- **pkg/metricstore**: `sanitizeStats(data *APIMetricData)` — zeros each independently
- **metricstoreclient**: `sanitizeStats(avg, min, max *schema.Float)` — zeros all if any is NaN. Also has `hasNaNStats()` helper.

### LoadData/LoadStats/LoadScopedStats/LoadNodeData/LoadNodeListData:
- Nearly identical result-processing logic
- Different data fetching: `FetchData(req)` vs `ccms.doRequest(ctx, &req)`  
- Internal store has `TestLoadDataCallback` test hook
- External client has topology caching

### Missing from pkg/metricstore:
- **MemoryDomain → Socket** scope transformation (present in metricstoreclient)

### Missing from metricstoreclient:
- Nothing significant missing from metricstoreclient

## Consolidation Approach

The user said metricstoreclient is the cleaner implementation. I agree — `buildScopeQueries()` as a standalone function is much cleaner than the inline if-chain.

Design a plan to:
1. Extract `buildScopeQueries()` as a shared helper (possibly in a shared package or as a package-level function in metricstore)
2. Synchronize the scope transformations (add MemoryDomain → Socket to internal)
3. Evaluate if result-processing logic can be shared
4. Consider type differences (int vs int64 Resolution)

Key constraints:
- The two packages have separate APIQuery types (different Resolution types, internal has ScaleFactor)
- The two packages use different data fetching mechanisms
- Must not break the MetricDataRepository interface contract
- Keep changes minimal and focused

Provide a detailed implementation plan.

     5→
     6→// Package metricstoreclient - Query Building
     7→//
     8→// This file contains the query construction and scope transformation logic for cc-metric-store queries.
     9→// It handles the complex mapping between requested metric scopes and native hardware topology,
    10→// automatically aggregating or filtering metrics as needed.
    11→//
    12→// # Scope Transformations
    13→//
    14→// The buildScopeQueries function implements the core scope transformation algorithm.
    15→// It handles 25+ different transformation cases, mapping between:
    16→//   - Accelerator (GPU) scope
    17→//   - HWThread (hardware thread/SMT) scope
    18→//   - Core (CPU core) scope
    19→//   - Socket (CPU package) scope
    20→//   - MemoryDomain (NUMA domain) scope
    21→//   - Node (full system) scope
    22→//
    23→// Transformations follow these rules:
    24→//   - Same scope: Return data as-is (e.g., Core → Core)
    25→//   - Coarser scope: Aggregate data (e.g., Core → Socket with Aggregate=true)
    26→//   - Finer scope: Error - cannot increase granularity
    27→//
    28→// # Query Building
    29→//
    30→// buildQueries and buildNodeQueries are the main entry points, handling job-specific
    31→// and node-specific query construction respectively. They:
    32→//   - Validate metric configurations
    33→//   - Handle subcluster-specific metric filtering
    34→//   - Detect and skip duplicate scope requests
    35→//   - Call buildScopeQueries for each metric/scope/host combination
    36→package metricstoreclient
    37→
    38→import (
    39→	"fmt"
    40→	"strconv"
    41→
    42→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    43→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    44→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    45→)
    46→
    47→// Scope string constants used in API queries.
    48→// Pre-converted to avoid repeated allocations during query building.
    49→var (
    50→	hwthreadString     = string(schema.MetricScopeHWThread)
    51→	coreString         = string(schema.MetricScopeCore)
    52→	memoryDomainString = string(schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain)
    53→	socketString       = string(schema.MetricScopeSocket)
    54→	acceleratorString  = string(schema.MetricScopeAccelerator)
    55→)
    56→
    57→// buildQueries constructs API queries for job-specific metric data.
    58→// It iterates through metrics, scopes, and job resources to build the complete query set.
    59→//
    60→// The function handles:
    61→//   - Metric configuration validation and subcluster filtering
    62→//   - Scope deduplication to avoid redundant queries
    63→//   - Hardware thread list resolution (job-allocated vs full node)
    64→//   - Delegation to buildScopeQueries for scope transformations
    65→//
    66→// Returns queries and their corresponding assigned scopes (which may differ from requested scopes).
    67→func (ccms *CCMetricStore) buildQueries(
    68→	job *schema.Job,
    69→	metrics []string,
    70→	scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    71→	resolution int,
    72→) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope, error) {
    73→	// Initialize both slices together
    74→	queries := make([]APIQuery, 0, len(metrics)*len(scopes)*len(job.Resources))
    75→	assignedScope := make([]schema.MetricScope, 0, len(metrics)*len(scopes)*len(job.Resources))
    76→
    77→	topology, err := ccms.getTopology(job.Cluster, job.SubCluster)
    78→	if err != nil {
    79→		cclog.Errorf("could not load cluster %s subCluster %s topology: %s", job.Cluster, job.SubCluster, err.Error())
    80→		return nil, nil, err
    81→	}
    82→
    83→	for _, metric := range metrics {
    84→		remoteName := metric
    85→		mc := archive.GetMetricConfig(job.Cluster, metric)
    86→		if mc == nil {
    87→			cclog.Warnf("metric '%s' is not specified for cluster '%s' - skipping", metric, job.Cluster)
    88→			continue
    89→		}
    90→
    91→		// Skip if metric is removed for subcluster
    92→		if len(mc.SubClusters) != 0 {
    93→			isRemoved := false
    94→			for _, scConfig := range mc.SubClusters {
    95→				if scConfig.Name == job.SubCluster && scConfig.Remove {
    96→					isRemoved = true
    97→					break
    98→				}
    99→			}
   100→			if isRemoved {
   101→				continue
   102→			}
   103→		}
   104→
   105→		// Avoid duplicates...
   106→		handledScopes := make([]schema.MetricScope, 0, 3)
   107→
   108→	scopesLoop:
   109→		for _, requestedScope := range scopes {
   110→			nativeScope := mc.Scope
   111→			if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && job.NumAcc == 0 {
   112→				continue
   113→			}
   114→
   115→			scope := nativeScope.Max(requestedScope)
   116→			for _, s := range handledScopes {
   117→				if scope == s {
   118→					continue scopesLoop
   119→				}
   120→			}
   121→			handledScopes = append(handledScopes, scope)
   122→
   123→			for _, host := range job.Resources {
   124→				hwthreads := host.HWThreads
   125→				if hwthreads == nil {
   126→					hwthreads = topology.Node
   127→				}
   128→
   129→				// Note: Expected exceptions will return as empty slices -> Continue
   130→				hostQueries, hostScopes := buildScopeQueries(
   131→					nativeScope, requestedScope,
   132→					remoteName, host.Hostname,
   133→					topology, hwthreads, host.Accelerators,
   134→					resolution,
   135→				)
   136→
   137→				// Note: Unexpected errors, such as unhandled cases, will return as nils -> Error
   138→				if hostQueries == nil && hostScopes == nil {
   139→					return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("METRICDATA/EXTERNAL-CCMS > TODO: unhandled case: native-scope=%s, requested-scope=%s", nativeScope, requestedScope)
   140→				}
   141→
   142→				queries = append(queries, hostQueries...)
   143→				assignedScope = append(assignedScope, hostScopes...)
   144→			}
   145→		}
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	return queries, assignedScope, nil
   149→}
   150→
   151→// buildNodeQueries constructs API queries for node-specific metric data (Systems View).
   152→// Similar to buildQueries but uses full node topology instead of job-allocated resources.
   153→//
   154→// The function handles:
   155→//   - SubCluster topology resolution (either pre-loaded or per-node lookup)
   156→//   - Full node hardware thread lists (not job-specific subsets)
   157→//   - All accelerators on each node
   158→//   - Metric configuration validation with subcluster filtering
   159→//
   160→// Returns queries and their corresponding assigned scopes.
   161→func (ccms *CCMetricStore) buildNodeQueries(
   162→	cluster string,
   163→	subCluster string,
   164→	nodes []string,
   165→	metrics []string,
   166→	scopes []schema.MetricScope,
   167→	resolution int,
   168→) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope, error) {
   169→	// Initialize both slices together
   170→	queries := make([]APIQuery, 0, len(metrics)*len(scopes)*len(nodes))
   171→	assignedScope := make([]schema.MetricScope, 0, len(metrics)*len(scopes)*len(nodes))
   172→
   173→	for _, metric := range metrics {
   174→		remoteName := metric
   175→		mc := archive.GetMetricConfig(cluster, metric)
   176→		if mc == nil {
   177→			cclog.Warnf("metric '%s' is not specified for cluster '%s'", metric, cluster)
   178→			continue
   179→		}
   180→
   181→		// Skip if metric is removed for subcluster
   182→		if mc.SubClusters != nil {
   183→			isRemoved := false
   184→			for _, scConfig := range mc.SubClusters {
   185→				if scConfig.Name == subCluster && scConfig.Remove {
   186→					isRemoved = true
   187→					break
   188→				}
   189→			}
   190→			if isRemoved {
   191→				continue
   192→			}
   193→		}
   194→
   195→		// Avoid duplicates...
   196→		handledScopes := make([]schema.MetricScope, 0, 3)
   197→
   198→	scopesLoop:
   199→		for _, requestedScope := range scopes {
   200→			nativeScope := mc.Scope
   201→
   202→			scope := nativeScope.Max(requestedScope)
   203→			for _, s := range handledScopes {
   204→				if scope == s {
   205→					continue scopesLoop
   206→				}
   207→			}
   208→			handledScopes = append(handledScopes, scope)
   209→
   210→			for _, hostname := range nodes {
   211→				var topology *schema.Topology
   212→				var err error
   213→
   214→				// If no subCluster given, get it by node
   215→				if subCluster == "" {
   216→					topology, err = ccms.getTopologyByNode(cluster, hostname)
   217→				} else {
   218→					topology, err = ccms.getTopology(cluster, subCluster)
   219→				}
   220→
   221→				if err != nil {
   222→					return nil, nil, err
   223→				}
   224→
   225→				// Always full node hwthread id list, no partial queries expected -> Use "topology.Node" directly where applicable
   226→				// Always full accelerator id list, no partial queries expected -> Use "acceleratorIds" directly where applicable
   227→				acceleratorIds := topology.GetAcceleratorIDs()
   228→
   229→				// Moved check here if metric matches hardware specs
   230→				if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && len(acceleratorIds) == 0 {
   231→					continue scopesLoop
   232→				}
   233→
   234→				nodeQueries, nodeScopes := buildScopeQueries(
   235→					nativeScope, requestedScope,
   236→					remoteName, hostname,
   237→					topology, topology.Node, acceleratorIds,
   238→					resolution,
   239→				)
   240→
   241→				if len(nodeQueries) == 0 && len(nodeScopes) == 0 {
   242→					return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("METRICDATA/EXTERNAL-CCMS > TODO: unhandled case: native-scope=%s, requested-scope=%s", nativeScope, requestedScope)
   243→				}
   244→
   245→				queries = append(queries, nodeQueries...)
   246→				assignedScope = append(assignedScope, nodeScopes...)
   247→			}
   248→		}
   249→	}
   250→
   251→	return queries, assignedScope, nil
   252→}
   253→
   254→// buildScopeQueries generates API queries for a given scope transformation.
   255→// It returns a slice of queries and corresponding assigned scopes.
   256→// Some transformations (e.g., HWThread -> Core/Socket) may generate multiple queries.
   257→func buildScopeQueries(
   258→	nativeScope, requestedScope schema.MetricScope,
   259→	metric, hostname string,
   260→	topology *schema.Topology,
   261→	hwthreads []int,
   262→	accelerators []string,
   263→	resolution int,
   264→) ([]APIQuery, []schema.MetricScope) {
   265→	scope := nativeScope.Max(requestedScope)
   266→	queries := []APIQuery{}
   267→	scopes := []schema.MetricScope{}
   268→
   269→	hwthreadsStr := intToStringSlice(hwthreads)
   270→
   271→	// Accelerator -> Accelerator (Use "accelerator" scope if requested scope is lower than node)
   272→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && scope.LT(schema.MetricScopeNode) {
   273→		if scope != schema.MetricScopeAccelerator {
   274→			// Expected Exception -> Continue -> Return Empty Slices
   275→			return queries, scopes
   276→		}
   277→
   278→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   279→			Metric:     metric,
   280→			Hostname:   hostname,
   281→			Aggregate:  false,
   282→			Type:       &acceleratorString,
   283→			TypeIds:    accelerators,
   284→			Resolution: resolution,
   285→		})
   286→		scopes = append(scopes, schema.MetricScopeAccelerator)
   287→		return queries, scopes
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	// Accelerator -> Node
   291→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeAccelerator && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   292→		if len(accelerators) == 0 {
   293→			// Expected Exception -> Continue -> Return Empty Slices
   294→			return queries, scopes
   295→		}
   296→
   297→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   298→			Metric:     metric,
   299→			Hostname:   hostname,
   300→			Aggregate:  true,
   301→			Type:       &acceleratorString,
   302→			TypeIds:    accelerators,
   303→			Resolution: resolution,
   304→		})
   305→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   306→		return queries, scopes
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	// HWThread -> HWThread
   310→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread {
   311→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   312→			Metric:     metric,
   313→			Hostname:   hostname,
   314→			Aggregate:  false,
   315→			Type:       &hwthreadString,
   316→			TypeIds:    hwthreadsStr,
   317→			Resolution: resolution,
   318→		})
   319→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   320→		return queries, scopes
   321→	}
   322→
   323→	// HWThread -> Core
   324→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeCore {
   325→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   326→		for _, core := range cores {
   327→			queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   328→				Metric:     metric,
   329→				Hostname:   hostname,
   330→				Aggregate:  true,
   331→				Type:       &hwthreadString,
   332→				TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(topology.Core[core]),
   333→				Resolution: resolution,
   334→			})
   335→			scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   336→		}
   337→		return queries, scopes
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	// HWThread -> Socket
   341→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   342→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   343→		for _, socket := range sockets {
   344→			queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   345→				Metric:     metric,
   346→				Hostname:   hostname,
   347→				Aggregate:  true,
   348→				Type:       &hwthreadString,
   349→				TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(topology.Socket[socket]),
   350→				Resolution: resolution,
   351→			})
   352→			scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   353→		}
   354→		return queries, scopes
   355→	}
   356→
   357→	// HWThread -> Node
   358→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeHWThread && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   359→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   360→			Metric:     metric,
   361→			Hostname:   hostname,
   362→			Aggregate:  true,
   363→			Type:       &hwthreadString,
   364→			TypeIds:    hwthreadsStr,
   365→			Resolution: resolution,
   366→		})
   367→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   368→		return queries, scopes
   369→	}
   370→
   371→	// Core -> Core
   372→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeCore {
   373→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   374→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   375→			Metric:     metric,
   376→			Hostname:   hostname,
   377→			Aggregate:  false,
   378→			Type:       &coreString,
   379→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(cores),
   380→			Resolution: resolution,
   381→		})
   382→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   383→		return queries, scopes
   384→	}
   385→
   386→	// Core -> Socket
   387→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   388→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromCores(hwthreads)
   389→		for _, socket := range sockets {
   390→			queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   391→				Metric:     metric,
   392→				Hostname:   hostname,
   393→				Aggregate:  true,
   394→				Type:       &coreString,
   395→				TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(topology.Socket[socket]),
   396→				Resolution: resolution,
   397→			})
   398→			scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   399→		}
   400→		return queries, scopes
   401→	}
   402→
   403→	// Core -> Node
   404→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeCore && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   405→		cores, _ := topology.GetCoresFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   406→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   407→			Metric:     metric,
   408→			Hostname:   hostname,
   409→			Aggregate:  true,
   410→			Type:       &coreString,
   411→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(cores),
   412→			Resolution: resolution,
   413→		})
   414→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   415→		return queries, scopes
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	// MemoryDomain -> MemoryDomain
   419→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain {
   420→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   421→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   422→			Metric:     metric,
   423→			Hostname:   hostname,
   424→			Aggregate:  false,
   425→			Type:       &memoryDomainString,
   426→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(memDomains),
   427→			Resolution: resolution,
   428→		})
   429→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   430→		return queries, scopes
   431→	}
   432→
   433→	// MemoryDomain -> Node
   434→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   435→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   436→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   437→			Metric:     metric,
   438→			Hostname:   hostname,
   439→			Aggregate:  true,
   440→			Type:       &memoryDomainString,
   441→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(memDomains),
   442→			Resolution: resolution,
   443→		})
   444→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   445→		return queries, scopes
   446→	}
   447→
   448→	// MemoryDomain -> Socket
   449→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeMemoryDomain && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   450→		memDomains, _ := topology.GetMemoryDomainsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   451→		socketToDomains, err := topology.GetMemoryDomainsBySocket(memDomains)
   452→		if err != nil {
   453→			cclog.Errorf("Error mapping memory domains to sockets, return unchanged: %v", err)
   454→			// Rare Error Case -> Still Continue -> Return Empty Slices
   455→			return queries, scopes
   456→		}
   457→
   458→		// Create a query for each socket
   459→		for _, domains := range socketToDomains {
   460→			queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   461→				Metric:     metric,
   462→				Hostname:   hostname,
   463→				Aggregate:  true,
   464→				Type:       &memoryDomainString,
   465→				TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(domains),
   466→				Resolution: resolution,
   467→			})
   468→			// Add scope for each query, not just once
   469→			scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   470→		}
   471→		return queries, scopes
   472→	}
   473→
   474→	// Socket -> Socket
   475→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeSocket && scope == schema.MetricScopeSocket {
   476→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   477→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   478→			Metric:     metric,
   479→			Hostname:   hostname,
   480→			Aggregate:  false,
   481→			Type:       &socketString,
   482→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(sockets),
   483→			Resolution: resolution,
   484→		})
   485→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   486→		return queries, scopes
   487→	}
   488→
   489→	// Socket -> Node
   490→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeSocket && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   491→		sockets, _ := topology.GetSocketsFromHWThreads(hwthreads)
   492→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   493→			Metric:     metric,
   494→			Hostname:   hostname,
   495→			Aggregate:  true,
   496→			Type:       &socketString,
   497→			TypeIds:    intToStringSlice(sockets),
   498→			Resolution: resolution,
   499→		})
   500→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   501→		return queries, scopes
   502→	}
   503→
   504→	// Node -> Node
   505→	if nativeScope == schema.MetricScopeNode && scope == schema.MetricScopeNode {
   506→		queries = append(queries, APIQuery{
   507→			Metric:     metric,
   508→			Hostname:   hostname,
   509→			Resolution: resolution,
   510→		})
   511→		scopes = append(scopes, scope)
   512→		return queries, scopes
   513→	}
   514→
   515→	// Unhandled Case -> Error -> Return nils
   516→	return nil, nil
   517→}
   518→
   519→// intToStringSlice converts a slice of integers to a slice of strings.
   520→// Used to convert hardware IDs (core IDs, socket IDs, etc.) to the string format required by the API.
   521→func intToStringSlice(is []int) []string {
   522→	ss := make([]string, len(is))
   523→	for i, x := range is {
   524→		ss[i] = strconv.Itoa(x)
   525→	}
   526→	return ss
   527→}

    21→type MetricDataRepository interface {
    22→	// Return the JobData for the given job, only with the requested metrics.
    23→	LoadData(job *schema.Job,
    24→		metrics []string,
    25→		scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    26→		ctx context.Context,
    27→		resolution int) (schema.JobData, error)
    28→
    29→	// Return a map of metrics to a map of nodes to the metric statistics of the job. node scope only.
    30→	LoadStats(job *schema.Job,
    31→		metrics []string,
    32→		ctx context.Context) (map[string]map[string]schema.MetricStatistics, error)
    33→
    34→	// Return a map of metrics to a map of scopes to the scoped metric statistics of the job.
    35→	LoadScopedStats(job *schema.Job,
    36→		metrics []string,
    37→		scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    38→		ctx context.Context) (schema.ScopedJobStats, error)
    39→
    40→	// Return a map of hosts to a map of metrics at the requested scopes (currently only node) for that node.
    41→	LoadNodeData(cluster string,
    42→		metrics, nodes []string,
    43→		scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    44→		from, to time.Time,
    45→		ctx context.Context) (map[string]map[string][]*schema.JobMetric, error)
    46→
    47→	// Return a map of hosts to a map of metrics to a map of scopes for multiple nodes.
    48→	LoadNodeListData(cluster, subCluster string,
    49→		nodes []string,
    50→		metrics []string,
    51→		scopes []schema.MetricScope,
    52→		resolution int,
    53→		from, to time.Time,
    54→		ctx context.Context) (map[string]schema.JobData, error)
    55→
    56→	// HealthCheck evaluates the monitoring state for a set of nodes against expected metrics.
    57→	HealthCheck(cluster string,
    58→		nodes []string,
    59→		metrics []string) (map[string]metricstore.HealthCheckResult, error)
    60→}
    61→
    62→type CCMetricStoreConfig struct {
    63→	Scope string `json:"scope"`
    64→	URL   string `json:"url"`
    65→	Token string `json:"token"`
    66→}
    67→
    68→var metricDataRepos map[string]MetricDataRepository = map[string]MetricDataRepository{}
    69→
    70→func Init(rawConfig json.RawMessage) error {
    71→	if rawConfig != nil {
    72→		var configs []CCMetricStoreConfig
    73→		config.Validate(configSchema, rawConfig)
    74→		dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(rawConfig))
    75→		dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
    76→		if err := dec.Decode(&configs); err != nil {
    77→			return fmt.Errorf("[METRICDISPATCH]> External Metric Store Config Init: Could not decode config file '%s' Error: %s", rawConfig, err.Error())
    78→		}
    79→
    80→		if len(configs) == 0 {
    81→			return fmt.Errorf("[METRICDISPATCH]> No external metric store configurations found in config file")
    82→		}
    83→
    84→		for _, config := range configs {
    85→			metricDataRepos[config.Scope] = ccms.NewCCMetricStore(config.URL, config.Token)
    86→		}
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	return nil
    90→}
    91→
    92→func GetMetricDataRepo(cluster string, subcluster string) (MetricDataRepository, error) {
    93→	var repo MetricDataRepository
    94→	var ok bool
    95→
    96→	key := cluster + "-" + subcluster
    97→	repo, ok = metricDataRepos[key]
    98→
    99→	if !ok {
   100→		repo, ok = metricDataRepos[cluster]
   101→
   102→		if !ok {
   103→			repo, ok = metricDataRepos["*"]
   104→
   105→			if !ok {
   106→				if metricstore.MetricStoreHandle == nil {
   107→					return nil, fmt.Errorf("[METRICDISPATCH]> no metric data repository configured '%s'", key)
   108→				}
   109→
   110→				repo = metricstore.MetricStoreHandle
   111→				cclog.Debugf("[METRICDISPATCH]> Using internal metric data repository for '%s'", key)
   112→			}
   113→		}
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	return repo, nil
   117→}
   118→
   119→// GetHealthCheckRepo returns the MetricDataRepository for performing health checks on a cluster.
   120→// It uses the same fallback logic as GetMetricDataRepo: cluster → wildcard → internal.
   121→func GetHealthCheckRepo(cluster string) (MetricDataRepository, error) {
   122→	return GetMetricDataRepo(cluster, "")
   123→}
